title: Lagrane
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image= |name=Lagrane |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You act like you never saw anyone frag before.''"
"''Never a wingman, sir.''"
"''It happens, captain. Let it go. Learn from it and go on. Or next time, it could be you.|Lagrane and [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]]|}} '''Lagrane''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] aboard the [[MC80A Home One Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Home One'' type Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. ==Biography== In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Lagrane served as one of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna's]] staff officers on the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']]. Bearing the insignia of a [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|lieutenant commander]], Lagrane met with [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Training officer/Legends|training officer]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wex Dafid]], delivering Dodonna's order that [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] be transferred to active duty in [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]]. Now-Commander Lagrane served as Farlander's debriefing officer for his many engagements with Red Squadron, receiving the pilot's [[After-action report/Legends|after-action reports]] and analyzing his performance from the campaign against [[Operation Strike Fear]] to the Rebellion's [[Operation Skyhook]], up until the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Following Farlander's involvement in a successful [[Rescue of Sian Tevv|rescue mission]], Lagrane promoted him to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]], offering the pilot a set of captain's epaulets and an uncharacteristic smile. Near the end of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Operation Strike Fear, Commander Lagrane was present for a briefing planning the [[Destruction of the Invincible|destruction of the operation's flagship]], asking how the Alliance would secure the explosive material necessary, and leading Commander [[H. S'man|S'man]] to outline an upcoming mission to [[Capture of the Gafra|recover]] it from the [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] ''[[Gafra]]''. After Captain Farlander prevented a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] replacement on the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']], Lagrane recommended that he take some time off, citing a lack of concentration. However, Farlander returned to active duty from the medical bay through the [[Mind trick/Legends|use of the Force]], going on to [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|destroy the ''Intrepid'']], after which Lagrane informed him of his friend and fellow pilot [[Sunnar Jan-lo]]'s survival. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Keyan Farlander's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] and close friend [[Breth Gart]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in [[Battle of Kalla VII|battle]]. Recognizing the impact of Gart's loss on Farlander, Lagrane recommended him for a psychological evaluation. Unwilling to be removed from duty, Farlander again used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] first on Commander S'man, and then on Lagrane himself to maintain his active status. Lagrane was transferred to [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] during the build up to the Battle of Yavin along with much of the ''Independence''{{'s}} personnel, including Captain Farlander. After destroying the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star's]] [[communications satellite]], Farlander attempted to apologize to Lagrane for his use of a mind trick on him, an action he had come to regret. ==Personality and traits== A typically dour and serious man, Lagrane had a military bearing and rarely laughed or smiled. Nevertheless, he would offer rare praise or a smirk at a particularly excellent performance from the pilots under his command, such as Keyan Farlander or Breth Gart. Despite his stern exterior, Lagrane was invested in the wellbeing of his pilots, complimenting them on their successes and comforting in their failures, though he grew frustrated at Farlander's repeated losses of wingmen, and found himself out of his depth then discussing the pilot's [[Force vision/Legends|troubling dreams]]. Lagrane showed susceptibility to Force mind tricks. ==Behind the scenes== The game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] delivers debriefing on a screen, with no officer appearing.[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] Lagrane was established as Farlander's debriefing officer in ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''. ==Sources== *''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Alliance Military officers]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Males]]
